---
title: "AI Regulation: A Comparative Policy Brief"
subtitle: "EU AI Act · NIST AI RMF · Korea AI Act"
author: "Acme Policy Practice"
date: "2026-05-08"
format:
  html: { toc: true }
  pdf: { documentclass: article, geometry: margin=1in, fontsize: 11pt }
---

## Executive Summary {.unnumbered}

> **Recommendation.** Korean tech companies exporting AI-enabled products to the EU should adopt EU AI Act risk-classification by Q3 2026 to reduce certification friction. NIST AI RMF (US) is voluntary but rapidly becoming a procurement default for federal contracts.

| Jurisdiction | Risk Tiers | Penalties | Effective | Mandatory |
|--------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
| EU AI Act    | 4 (unacceptable / high / limited / minimal) | up to €35M or 7% global turnover | Aug 2026 | Yes |
| US NIST RMF  | n/a (function-based)  | n/a (voluntary) | Active   | Procurement de-facto |
| KR AI Act    | 3 (high / limited / general)  | up to ₩300M | Jul 2026 | Yes |

## Comparative Analysis

### Risk Classification Approaches

The EU's 4-tier system is the most prescriptive, banning "unacceptable risk" applications outright (e.g., social scoring, real-time biometric ID in public spaces with narrow exceptions). Korea's 3-tier system aligns broadly but uses different category names. The US RMF is principles-based and does not classify by risk tier.

### Compliance Cost (Indicative)

For a mid-sized AI vendor (~$10M ARR):
- EU AI Act high-risk: $400K–$1.2M one-time + $200K/yr ongoing
- KR AI Act high-risk: ₩200M–₩600M one-time
- NIST RMF: ~$100K consultancy if seeking federal contracts

### Enforcement Maturity

EU has appointed national AI authorities; first enforcement actions expected Q4 2026. Korea's MSIT has issued draft guidance; full enforcement Q1 2027. NIST RMF remains advisory at federal level; states (CO, CA) have begun mandating elements.

## Recommendation Matrix

| If your product is... | Then prioritize |
|------------------------|-----------------|
| EU-bound, high-risk    | EU AI Act conformity assessment (Q3 deadline) |
| US federal-bound       | NIST RMF mapping + contract clauses |
| KR domestic only       | KR AI Act tier-2 documentation |
| Multi-jurisdiction     | EU AI Act baseline (most prescriptive) |

## Appendix A — EU AI Act Tier Definitions

(Detailed regulatory text mapped to product categories.)

## Appendix B — Korean AI Act Translation

(Original Korean text + working English translation, side-by-side.)

## Appendix C — NIST RMF Function Crosswalk

GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, MANAGE functions mapped to ISO 42001 controls.

## Appendix D — Compliance Cost Methodology

Based on 12 vendor interviews + EU Commission impact assessment.
